message:
fix(ci): Use correct WAMR instruction metering flag name

The CMake flag should be WAMR_BUILD_INSTRUCTION_METERING, not
WAMR_ENABLE_INSTRUCTION_METERING. This was causing the flag to be
ignored during build, resulting in the instruction metering feature
not being compiled into WAMR.

This fixes the undefined symbol error:
  wasm_runtime_set_instruction_count_limit

References:
- vendor/wamr/product-mini/platforms/darwin/CMakeLists.txt:106
- CI log showed CMake warning about unused variable

files.patch:
@@ -29,7 +29,7 @@ cd "${BUILD_DIR}"
 cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
     -DWAMR_BUILD_FAST_JIT=0 \
     -DWAMR_BUILD_SIMD=1 \
-    -DWAMR_ENABLE_INSTRUCTION_METERING=1
+    -DWAMR_BUILD_INSTRUCTION_METERING=1
 
 # Build with all available cores
 make -j$(nproc 2>/dev/null || sysctl -n hw.ncpu 2>/dev/null || echo 4)
